telltalePatterns: h-deletion in word-initial position, evidenced by "house" /aʊs/
Substitution of dental fricatives /θ, ð/ with alveolar stops /t, d/, evidenced by "think" /tɪŋk/ and "weather" /wɛdər/
Non-rhoticity and L1-influenced (uvular) realization of /r/ for English /ɹ/, evidenced by "river" /ʁivər/

prosody.intonation: L1-influenced, with narrower pitch range and flatter contours
prosody.fluencyNotes: Generally fluent with natural pausing at phrase boundaries. Connected speech is present but not as extensive as a native speaker, with word boundaries often clearer.

strengths: Clear articulation of most consonant sounds, including /s/, /z/, /f/, /v/, /m/, /n/, /p/, /b/, /k/, /g/.
Good overall speech rate and natural pausing at phrase boundaries, contributing to clear phrasing.
Generally accurate lexical stress placement, even if stress prominence is reduced.

recommendedFocus.reason: Consistent substitution with alveolar stops /t, d/ significantly impacts clarity and marks a prominent foreign accent. Remediation would greatly improve intelligibility.
Consistent deletion of /h/ can lead to word confusion (e.g., 'ear' vs 'hear') and is a strong indicator of L1 interference.
Developing a more stress-timed rhythm and native-like intonation patterns would significantly enhance naturalness and overall comprehensibility, making the speech flow more natively.

result.transcript: Yesterday the weather was really beautiful. So I decided to go for a walk through the park near my house. The trees were green and the birds were singing everywhere. I think spring is my favorite time of the year. I walked along the river and watched some children playing with a big red ball. They were laughing and having so much fun together. One little girl threw the ball very high and it landed right in the water. Her father had to reach in and pull it out. After that I stopped at a small village shop to buy some bread and a bottle of juice.
